comment: Per-scenario regression config for tests/Aws2Azure.PerfTests. Two gates: (1) ABSOLUTE floors/ceilings in 'scenarios' (minThroughputPerSec / maxP99Ms), enforced per-test by PerfResult.AssertNoRegression — either field 0 opts out of that half; kept as wide catastrophe-detectors on emulator-bound paths whose azure-sdk.* baseline is absent OR too unstable to be a ruler (the Service Bus emulator's own SDK send p50 swings 3-5x run-to-run because it link-attaches per send while the proxy pools connections, so sqs.SendMessage / sns.Publish are gated on a THROUGHPUT FLOOR ONLY (their p99 is bimodal on the proxy side too — a rare cold AMQP link-attach lands a multi-second spike in the top 1% unpredictably, observed 234 ms one run and 3108 ms the next — so any absolute p99 ceiling flaps just like the relative gate; the throughput floor plus AssertHealthy is the only stable catastrophe signal)). dynamodb.BatchGetItem is likewise absolute-gated (maxP99Ms 700) not paired: there the PROXY p99 is the stable side (~240-340 ms, unimodal Cosmos REST) while the azure-sdk.Cosmos.ReadManyItems baseline is the jittery, structurally-unfair ruler (a specialized batched SDK API the proxy implements as N point-reads; its p99 collapsed 106->45 ms one run, spiking the ratio to 5.3x with no proxy change). (2) RELATIVE proxy-vs-baseline ratios in 'pairings', enforced once per run by RelativeRegressionGateTests (Category=RelativeGate) reading docs/perf/baseline-latest.json. The relative gate is the meaningful signal where the baseline is STABLE: emulator tail-latency jitter hits BOTH the proxy and its azure-sdk.* baseline, so the ratio cancels the noise and fails only when the PROXY adds overhead. METRIC BY SHAPE: REST + AMQP-receive pairs gate on p99-ratio (unimodal, stable baseline); the EventHubs send pair (kinesis.PutRecord) gates on p50-ratio (EH baseline is stable at 3-5ms; p99 would be cold-link-attach noise). A 0 ratio opts out of that dimension. azure-sdk.* baselines are reference rulers — {0,0} absolute, never fail the build. Operators bump these deliberately when a proxy change is expected to move performance. EVERY scenario must have a 'scenarios' entry — KnownPerfScenariosTests asserts coverage.
